meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Project Management to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with unfixable bugs. Learn how systematic project management with Antigravity and Claude Code helps freelancers ship faster and error-free.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just code; it's a structural failure. By implementing rigorous project management, you isolate logic gates, map dependencies, and use AI-driven diagnostics to pinpoint root causes. Combining Antigravity's architectural oversight with Claude Code's execution power transforms chaotic debugging into a repeatable, automated engineering workflow that saves hours.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your codebase has devolved into a labyrinth of 'spooky action at a distance.' You’ve been staring at the same console error for three hours, and every patch creates two new regressions. This is the freelance death spiral: technical debt masquerading as bad luck.

But the breakthrough isn't in your syntax—it's in your process. Top-tier engineers don't 'hunt' bugs; they engineer them out of existence through strict project management and AI-assisted observability. It’s time to stop guessing and start architecting your way out of the chaos.

steps.title: Establish the Debugging Boundary
steps.description: Define the exact scope of the failure. Don't let the AI guess; provide a clear boundary of where the data enters the system and where it breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase for data flow bottlenecks. Identify the specific module where the current runtime error originates.

steps.title: Map Dependency Trees
steps.description: Most bugs are cross-module conflicts. Map your dependencies to see what changed recently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dependency map for the failing function and highlight recent changes in imported packages.

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Failure
steps.description: Create a minimal reproduction script. If you can't isolate it, you can't fix it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a unit test that isolates the failing logic block into a standalone environment.

steps.title: Execute Automated Diagnostics
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run the reproduction script and parse the stack trace for hidden anomalies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the test failure output and propose three potential architectural refactors to prevent this.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Patches
steps.description: Apply the fix and verify that no regressions were introduced to the wider system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply the proposed fix and write a regression test to ensure future stability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Fix: Resolved runtime memory leak in data-processor'

pro_tips: Treat every bug report as a documentation requirement; if you can't document it, the code isn't ready for production.
Use Claude Code's 'review' flag on every PR to catch logic flaws before they reach the staging environment.
Maintain a 'Bug Ledger' in Antigravity to identify recurring patterns in your code that require architectural changes.
Always run a full regression suite after any patch, even if the change seems trivial; side effects are the silent killer of freelance projects.
Automate your environment setup so your local debugger mirrors your client's production environment exactly.
content.next_steps: Integrate these steps into your standard project kickoff checklist to preemptively block bugs before they emerge.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start building the future. Our community of elite freelancers is shipping faster, breaking less, and charging more by mastering these exact workflows. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
